reasoning: While the programmatic signal shows high content recall (0.946), the LLM fidelity reports identify a significant structural failure where a consistent 13-item agenda table is truncated after the 4th item. The visual score of 0.5 and the identification of manual dot leaders replacing structured table data indicate a loss of semantic integrity and accessibility. Given the 3x cost of a false positive, the discrepancy between the pipeline's auto-approval and the LLM's structural warnings requires human intervention.

concerns.analysis: The source document presents the entire agenda (from 'Call to Order' through item 13) in a three-column tabular format (Tab, Topic, Presenter). The JSON output correctly identifies items 1-4 as a table but truncates the table structure thereafter. Items 5 through 13 are converted into standard paragraphs and lists, merging the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' column data into single text strings. This destroys the structural relationship between agenda items and their assigned presenters.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 5 and 6 on Page 1, and Items 7-13 on Page 2, align with the 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' column headers.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF maintains a consistent, unified columnar layout for all agenda items (1-13), with distinct columns for 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. The HTML output breaks this consistency by rendering items 1-4 in a structured table, but then switching to mixed list and paragraph formats for items 5-13. This fragmenting of the document's primary structure significantly changes its visual organization. Furthermore, in Item 5 (sections b-e), multiple lines of financial data from the source (Hea
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 5b in the source PDF lists 'NG 911 Fund/Account', 'April 2020 Month-End Balance', and 'April 2020 Disbursement' on separate lines with aligned dollar amounts. Items 7-13 are visually aligned with the 'Presenter' column on the right.
concerns.output_quote: Item 5b is rendered as a single line: '2. b) NG 911 Fund/Account April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707 April 2020 Disbursement $ 65,036'. Items 7-13 lose their columnar presenter alignment and use plain text dot leaders instead.

concerns.analysis: The agenda items in the source PDF are presented in a tabular format with defined headers ('Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter'). While the HTML output correctly initiates a table for the first few items, it terminates the table prematurely after item 4. Items 5 through 13, which are part of the same tabular structure in the source, are rendered as plain text paragraphs and lists. Specifically, items 7-13 are rendered as plain text with manual dot leaders, failing the requirement to maintain tabular data
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The entire agenda (items 1-13) is organized under the headers 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML table only contains items up to '4. Ceremonial Swearing-In...'. Items 7-13 are rendered as: '7. Education Committee Report.....................................Chuck Greene/Angie Turbeville'.
concerns.location: Items 5 through 13; second page of HTML rendering.

action_items: Restore the three-column table structure (Tab, Topic, Presenter) for agenda items 5 through 13
Fix financial data formatting in Item 5 (sections b-e) to ensure data is not merged into single strings
Remove manual dot leaders used in the plain text sections and replace with proper CSS/table formatting
